questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text: Define the term ‘hydrocarbon’.
questions.mark_scheme: (molecules) made up of carbon and hydrogen (atoms only)

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
The general formula for alkanes is CnH2n+2
Determine the formula of the alkane with 10 carbon atoms.
Formula = ____

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Crude oil is a mixture of hydrocarbons. 
Figure 1 represents industrial equipment used to separate crude oil into fractions.
Figure 1
Explain how crude oil is separated into fractions.
Use Figure 1.
questions.mark_scheme:
(crude oil) is heated
(so some of the hydrocarbons) vaporise
the column has a temperature gradient
(so) the hydrocarbons / fractions condense at different heights / temperatures in the column
or
(and) the hydrocarbons / fractions condense at their boiling points
questions.extra_notes:
allow the column gets cooler 
going up

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
The alkane molecule C14H30 can be cracked to produce smaller molecules.
Balance the equation for the reaction.
C14H30 → C8H18 + C3H6

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Propene (C3H6) is an alkene.
Describe the test for alkenes.
Give the result.
Test
____
Result
____
questions.mark_scheme:
add bromine (water)
(bromine water) changes (from orange) to colourless
questions.extra_notes:
allow (bromine water) 
decolourises 
ignore clear
MP2 is dependent on MP1 
being awarded

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Poly(propene) is made from propene.
Figure 2 represents the repeating unit of poly(propene).
Figure 2
What type of substance is poly(propene)?
questions.mark_scheme: polymer

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Some factors affect the rates of chemical reactions.
A student investigated the effect of changing the particle size of calcium carbonate on the rate of reaction with hydrochloric acid.
Figure 3 shows the apparatus.
Figure 3
Describe a method the student could use to produce valid results.
questions.mark_scheme:
Level 3: The method would lead to the production of a valid outcome. The key steps are identified and logically sequenced.
Level 2: The method would not necessarily lead to a valid outcome. Most steps are identified, but the method is not fully logically sequenced.
Level 1: The method would not lead to a valid outcome. Some relevant steps are identified, but links are not made clear.
No relevant content
Indicative content
• measure volume of (hydrochloric) acid
• using a measuring cylinder
• measure mass of calcium carbonate
• using a balance
• add (hydrochloric) acid to calcium carbonate in conical flask
• put stopper and delivery tube into conical flask
• start a timer
• record volume of gas collected at set time intervals
or
time how long it takes for a fixed volume of gas to be collected
• repeat using different sized pieces of calcium carbonate
• use same mass of calcium carbonate
• use same volume of (hydrochloric) acid
• use same concentration of (hydrochloric) acid
• use same temperature of (hydrochloric) acid
• repeat each experiment

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
The student investigated the effect of increasing the temperature on the rate of a reaction.
Explain the effect of increasing the temperature on the rate of a reaction.
Refer to particles and collisions in your answer.
questions.mark_scheme:
(increasing the temperature) increases the rate of reaction
(because) particles have more energy
(so) the frequency of collisions increases
questions.extra_notes:
allow (because) particles move 
faster
allow (so) a greater proportion of 
collisions have enough energy 
to react

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text: What is meant by a ‘catalyst’?
questions.mark_scheme:
(a substance that) increases the rate of reaction
and is not used up during the reaction
questions.extra_notes:
allow (a catalyst) increases / 
changes the rate of reaction 
ignore does not take part in the 
reaction

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text: What are catalysts in biological systems called?
questions.mark_scheme: enzymes

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Some fractions of crude oil are processed to produce fuel for transport.
Describe how crude oil was formed.
questions.mark_scheme:
plankton (died)
and were buried (in mud)
and were compressed
over millions of years
questions.extra_notes:
allow (remains of) ancient 
biomass
allow and were buried (by 
sediments)

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text: Suggest how sulfur dioxide can be produced by transport.
questions.mark_scheme:
(some) fuels contain sulfur
(which) reacts with oxygen to produce sulfur dioxide
questions.extra_notes:
allow a named fossil fuel for 
fuels

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Give two problems caused by sulfur dioxide as an atmospheric pollutant.
1 ____
2 ____
questions.mark_scheme:
acid rain
respiratory problems (in humans)
questions.extra_notes:
allow a specific effect of acid 
rain 
allow named respiratory 
problem eg asthma

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text: Describe how carbon monoxide can be produced by transport.
questions.mark_scheme:
fuels are burnt
in insufficient / limited oxygen
questions.extra_notes:
allow a named fossil fuel for 
fuels
allow (carbon monoxide is)
produced by incomplete 
combustion

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Catalytic converters are fitted to car exhausts to reduce the amount of pollution from cars.
Carbon monoxide and nitrogen dioxide (NO2) react in a catalytic converter.
Nitrogen and carbon dioxide are produced.
Write a balanced equation for the reaction.
____ + ____ → ____ + ____
questions.mark_scheme:
4 CO + 2 NO2 → N2 + 4 CO2
allow for 1 mark
CO + NO2 → N2 + CO2
with no / incorrect balancing numbers

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Printer ink is a mixture of chemicals.
A student used chromatography to investigate the colours in a printer ink.
The student put a spot of the printer ink on the start line.
Figure 4 shows the results.
Figure 4
Explain why the red colour did not move from the start line.
questions.mark_scheme:
red colour is insoluble (in the solvent)
(so) does not move with the solvent

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
The blue colour moved 6.4 cm up the chromatogram.
The Rf value of the blue colour is 0.87
Calculate the distance moved by the solvent.
Give your answer to 2 significant figures.
Distance moved by the solvent (2 significant figures) = ____ cm
questions.mark_scheme:
Rf =
distance moved by substance
distance moved by solvent
= 0.87
(distance moved by solvent =)
6.4 / 0.87 = 7.356
= 7.4 (cm)
questions.extra_notes:
allow a correctly calculated 
answer to 2 significant figures 
using the values in the question

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
There were four colours in the printer ink.
Suggest one reason why only three colours were visible on the chromatogram.
questions.mark_scheme:
any one from:
• (the two dyes) may have the same Rf value (as the blue / yellow colour)
• (the two dyes) may have spots in the same place
• two of the colours may be insoluble
• the other colour may be white

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text: Suggest how the student could use chromatography to show there were four colours in the printer ink.
questions.mark_scheme: use a different solvent

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
What is the amount of energy transferred during the reverse reaction?

|   |   |
|---|---|

| [ ] | = 198 kJ/mol |
| [ ] | > 198 kJ/mol |

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:

What is the effect on the position of the equilibrium?

|---|---|
| [ ] | Equilibrium position shifts to the left |
| [ ] | Equilibrium position does not change |
| [ ] | Equilibrium position shifts to the right |
questions.mark_scheme: equilibrium position shifts to the right

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
The pressure is decreased.
What is the effect on the position of the equilibrium?
Tick () one box.
|   |   |
|---|---|
| [ ] | Equilibrium position shifts to the left |
| [ ] | Equilibrium position does not change |
| [ ] | Equilibrium position shifts to the right |
questions.mark_scheme: equilibrium position shifts to the left

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
The temperature is increased.
What is the effect on the position of the equilibrium?
Tick () one box.
|   |   |
|---|---|
| [ ] | Equilibrium position shifts to the left |
| [ ] | Equilibrium position does not change |
| [ ] | Equilibrium position shifts to the right |
questions.mark_scheme: equilibrium position shifts to the left

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
A catalyst is used in the reaction.
Suggest what effect the catalyst has on the position of the equilibrium.
Give one reason for your answer.
Effect ____
Reason ____
questions.mark_scheme:
(effect)
(equilibrium position) does not change
(reason)
increases the rate of the forward reaction and reverse reaction equally

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
A scientist measured how the number of moles of sulfur dioxide, oxygen and sulfur trioxide varied with time during the reaction.
Figure 5 shows the results.
Figure 5
Determine the time taken for the reaction to reach equilibrium.
Explain your answer.
Use Figure 5.
Time ____ s
Explanation ____
questions.mark_scheme:
210 (s)
lines become level / horizontal
(because) the rates of the forward reaction and reverse reaction are equal
or
(because) the number of moles (of all three gases) remain constant
questions.extra_notes:
allow a value in the range 205 to 
210 (s)

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Figure 6 shows the results for sulfur trioxide.
Figure 6
Determine the rate of reaction at 60 seconds.
Rate = ____ mol/s
questions.mark_scheme:
tangent drawn at 60 s
correct values for y step and x step from tangent
(rate =)
value for y step / value for x step
correct calculation of rate (mol/s)
questions.extra_notes:
allow correct use of an 
incorrectly drawn tangent 
allow a tolerance of ± ½ a small 
square for each coordinate 
allow correct use of incorrectly 
determined value(s) from the 
tangent for y step and/or x step

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
The Earth’s natural resources are used to manufacture useful products. One useful product is potable water.
Potable water can be produced from sea water by distillation.
Give one disadvantage of using distillation to produce potable water.
questions.mark_scheme: large amounts of energy required
questions.extra_notes:
ignore references to time and/or 
cost

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text: Describe one other method to produce potable water from sea water.
questions.mark_scheme:
reverse osmosis
using membranes

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
A student investigated the mass of dissolved solids in a 100 cm3 sample of sea water.
This is the method used.
1. Weigh an evaporating basin.
2. Measure 100 cm3 of sea water.
3. Pour the sea water into the evaporating basin.
4. Heat the evaporating basin.
5. Weigh the evaporating basin and contents.
6. Calculate the mass of dissolved solids in the sea water.
Explain how repeating steps 4 and 5 would improve this method.
questions.mark_scheme:
(heat) until the mass is constant
to ensure that all the water has evaporated

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
The total mass of dissolved solids in a 100 cm3 sample of sea water is 3.50 g.
The percentage of sodium chloride in the dissolved solids is 77.8%.
Calculate the mass of sodium chloride dissolved in the 100 cm3 sample of sea water.
Mass of sodium chloride = ____ g

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Biological methods are used to extract metal compounds from metal ores.
One method of producing copper from low-grade copper ores is by using bacteria.
The bacteria produce leachate solutions that contain copper compounds.
Give two methods that can be used to extract copper from these leachate solutions.
1 ____
2 ____
questions.mark_scheme:
displacement using (scrap) iron
electrolysis

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Phytomining uses plants to absorb metal compounds from low-grade ores.
Describe how the metal compounds are obtained from the plants.
questions.mark_scheme:
plants are harvested
and burned
to produce ash (that contains metal compounds)

questions.exam: AQA GCSE Combined Science: Chemistry Paper 2H, 2024
questions.question_text:
Nickel is produced by phytomining.
One hectare of plants produces 215 kg of nickel.
Determine the area required to produce 750 kg of nickel.
Give your answer in m2.
One hectare = 10 000 m2
Area required = ____ m2
questions.mark_scheme:
(area =)
750 / 215 = 3.49 (hectares)
(conversion = 3.49 × 10 000 =) 34 900 (m²)
alternative approach
(conversion = 215 / 10 000 = ) 0.0215 (kg/m²) (1)
(area =) 750 / 0.0215 (1)
= 34 900 (m²) (1)
questions.extra_notes:
allow 3.488372093 correctly 
rounded to at least 2 significant 
figures
allow 3.49 × 104 (m2)
allow a correct conversion of an 
incorrectly determined area
allow correct use of an incorrect 
conversion
